parsed_review.summary: CP00-623 is a descriptor-only Risk-A closeout pack (RP20.P05.M01.S11-S20, 10 units, micro 'Contract Draft') for the Data Room/VDR P05 fixture contract draft tail. Read-only inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms it is internally consistent and boundary-clean. Identity/plan binding match across manifest, baseline, registry (DATA_ROOM_VDR_CORE_CP623_*), contract projections, and the RP20 validate script (range, unit_count=10, upstream CP00-622, next CP00-624 / RP20.P05.M02.S01). Deliverable distribution security_audit:2 / implementation:3 / fixture:1 / test:3 / hermes_evidence:1 = 10 is correctly derived from row-title classification (verified 'Claude missing-test prompt' -> test, 'Audit hint case'+'Security trimming case' -> security_audit, 'Fixture manifest' -> fixture). Implementation is descriptor-only: each row carries descriptor_only/runtime_execution:false/raw_payload_included:false; the freeze chain plus CP623 no-write attestation set all writes_*/dispatches_*/executes_*_runtime/emits_hermes_runtime_receipt to false; validators.js enforces ~35 assertions including synthetic_only, no_real_data, no object-storage, and the authority boundary (claude_is_final_approval:false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust:false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true). Three tests and the validate script cover coverage, descriptor-only flags, contract-projection equality, and pack-to-pack handoff continuity (CP622->CP623->CP624). No secret/real-data patterns found in source. Claude remains read-only (request: read_only, tools Read/Grep/Glob, model claude-opus-4-8); Hermes appears only as deterministic descriptor evidence shape, not product runtime. CP178+ forbidden_review_evidence list is present. The pack correctly reports production_ready:false / runtime_ready:false (descriptor-only) and the 'descriptor_verified' flag is supported by the evidence. No P0/P1/P2 issues. Two P3 notes only: (1) command-evidence/adjudication/construction-inspection are in sequence-appropriate pending state and the 11 validation commands run in the harness's later final_closeout_validation step, outside read-only scope, so closeout remains contingent on those passing; (2) changed-file-scope.json lists this pack's own evidence dir under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, a minor classification overlap.

findings.title: Closeout validation/adjudication/inspection finalized in later harness steps, outside read-only review scope
findings.details: At this review step (read_only_claude_run_raw_capture) the pre-review evidence is correctly pending: command-evidence.json has commands:[] with gate_outcome 'pending_pack_evidence_creation', construction-inspection.json verdict is PENDING_REVIEW, adjudication.md is pending, and claude-review-result.json is pending_review. The 11 validation commands (npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, rp05:matter-core:validate, npm test, npm run validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) execute in the harness's later final_closeout_validation step and cannot be run in this read-only review. This PASS attests to code-level correctness and boundary integrity of the descriptor pack; it does not attest to command execution results.
findings.recommendation: Proceed with the documented hardening_sequence: ensure final_closeout_validation runs all 11 commands green and that adjudication/construction-inspection are regenerated from this receipt before commit. Do not flip production_ready until those gates pass and human final approval is recorded.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json lists this pack's own evidence directory under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-623/ (this pack's evidence directory) and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md appear in both untracked_implementation_files (lines 180-181) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 186-187). The cp00-623/ evidence dir is genuinely in-scope for this pack and is correctly captured in the manifest's worktree_transition_policy.active_pack_required_untracked_files, so its appearance under 'unrelated' is a minor classification overlap in the generated scope artifact, not a content defect. No implementation, boundary, or closeout-correctness impact.
findings.recommendation: Optionally tighten the changed-file-scope generator so a pack's own docs/closeout-packs/<pack>/ evidence dir is not also emitted under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, to avoid downstream-reviewer confusion. The authoritative manifest classification already treats it as in-scope.
